summary: Completed backend-only PHP regression validation for bootstrap include-order fix and fatal-error checks.

minor.issue: DB not configured in this workspace causes repeated '[admin-bootstrap-db-fail] Database connection not available' log entries; non-fatal and expected for this environment.

action_items: No blocker found for the sanitize() redeclare regression; keep current bootstrap include order.
If needed for deeper admin flow testing, configure DB credentials in target environment.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical parse/fatal issues found in current PHP files (272 files linted).
Bootstrap fix is effective: no 'Cannot redeclare sanitize()' observed in tested include paths.
Missing /app/memory/test_credentials.md should be added for future auth-focused test runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated requested regression points via PHP CLI includes with proper chdir for admin/member paths. setup/install HTML output on public/member includes is expected due to no DB config. No fatal/parse/redeclare errors observed.
rca of the issue: Root cause (already fixed by E1): _bootstrap.php loaded core/helpers.php before includes/config.php, both defining sanitize(), causing fatal redeclare. Current load order now initializes includes/config.php first and only conditionally loads optional core helper files using sentinel function checks, preventing duplicate function definitions.
